title: User-Satisfaction Surveys
concept: Collecting structured human feedback on agent performance to build alignment datasets.

exercises.prompt: What method do we use to verify if a task was completed successfully by checking final results directly?
exercises.explain: State-Based Validation checks the outcome by verifying the final state of the task.

cards.title: What are User-Satisfaction Surveys?
cards.body: User-satisfaction surveys are tools that help gather feedback from users about their experiences with a system or an agent.

cards.title: Definition of User-Satisfaction Surveys
cards.body: These surveys collect structured feedback to evaluate how well an AI agent meets user needs and expectations.

cards.title: Surveys as Heartbeats
cards.body: Think of user surveys like checking a patient's heartbeat to understand their health; they reveal how well the AI is performing and where it needs improvement.

cards.title: Example of a User-Satisfaction Survey Question
cards.body: "On a scale from 1 to 5, how satisfied were you with the AI's response to your request?" This gathers direct user feedback.

cards.title: Myth: Surveys are Unnecessary
cards.body: Some may think surveys are unneeded, but they provide essential insights that data alone can't capture.

cards.title: Visualizing Feedback
cards.body: Surveys can show trends in user satisfaction over time, helping identify areas for improvement.

exercises.prompt: What do user-satisfaction surveys help us collect?
exercises.explain: User-satisfaction surveys specifically focus on gathering feedback regarding user experiences.

exercises.prompt: User-satisfaction surveys only focus on the technical accuracy of AI agents.
exercises.explain: Surveys capture user experiences and satisfaction, not just technical accuracy.

exercises.prompt: User-satisfaction surveys gather __________ feedback on AI performance.
exercises.sentence: User-satisfaction surveys gather structured feedback on AI performance.
exercises.explain: Structured feedback refers to organized input from users, which is what surveys collect.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a user-satisfaction survey question?

options.text: How did the agent perform the task?
Rate your satisfaction with the agent's help from 1-5!
What was the final output of the agent?

exercises.explain: The second option directly asks the user to evaluate their satisfaction, which is the purpose of a user-satisfaction survey.

exercises.prompt: Why are user-satisfaction surveys important for AI agents?

options.text: They provide direct user feedback for improvement.
They are the only method for data collection.

exercises.explain: User-satisfaction surveys are crucial as they offer insights directly from users, helping agents align better with user expectations and improve performance.
